findings.message: Irreversible delete action with no confirmation step. A user who misclicks the Delete button immediately loses the item with no chance to cancel.
findings.suggestion: Add a confirmation prompt (e.g. window.confirm or a modal dialog) before issuing the DELETE request, or provide an undo affordance after deletion.

findings.message: Mutating action gives no in-flight or success/error feedback: the button is not disabled while the DELETE request is pending, there is no loading indicator, and fetch errors are silently ignored before window.location.reload() runs.
findings.suggestion: Track a pending state to disable the button and show a spinner while the request is in flight; surface success/error feedback (e.g. toast or inline message) instead of an unconditional page reload.

findings.message: window.location.reload() after delete is a disorienting full-page refresh — scroll position, focus, and any other UI state are lost, which is a confusing flow for what should be a localized list update.
findings.suggestion: Update the items list in place (e.g. lift state up or refetch via the existing fetchItems hook) instead of forcing a full page reload.
